what_it_does: Defines a global toast notifier: a module-level window._toastListeners array and a window.dsaToast(msg) function that fans a message to all listeners, plus a React `Toast` component that subscribes on mount, shows the message for 2200ms then clears it, and renders a ViShape glyph beside the text. Exposes window.Toast.
observations: Pub/sub via window._toastListeners (initialized with `|| []`) and window.dsaToast as the publish entry point.
Toast component holds local msg state; subscriber sets msg then setTimeout(2200) clears it.
Cleanup removes the subscriber from window._toastListeners on unmount.
Renders <div className='dsa-toast {show}'> with a ViShape size=14 and the message span.
Single shared instance model (header comment 'single shared instance').
standouts: Decouples emitters from the React tree via a plain window-global pub/sub (dsaToast can be called from anywhere, including non-React code).
conventions: Window-global registration (window.Toast, window.dsaToast) consistent with the app.
Composes the ViShape brand glyph rather than an icon font.
concerns: The 2200ms duration is a magic number, not tokenized.
No queueing: rapid successive dsaToast calls overwrite msg and reset the single timer, so messages can be lost or clipped.
Assumes a single mounted Toast; multiple instances would each show every message.
notes: A window-global toast: call window.dsaToast('...') from anywhere; one mounted <Toast/> shows it for 2.2s with the Vi glyph.
questions: Is exactly one <Toast/> guaranteed to be mounted, given the single-instance assumption?
Is the lack of a message queue intentional for this design surface?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: header comment 'simple global notifier (single shared instance)' is exactly the implemented behavior.
apparent_themes: Decoupling non-React emitters from React state
intention_signals: Aims to give any part of the studio (React or not) a one-call way to surface transient feedback, branded with the Vi mark, without prop-drilling a notifier.
novelty: Pairs a non-React window-global emit API with a React renderer, and brands the toast with the Vi diamond rather than a generic icon.
contribution: Contributes a window-global call-from-anywhere toast channel with a branded glyph that a fused system would keep as the transient-notification primitive.
summary_for_embedding: Global React toast notifier using a window._toastListeners pub/sub and window.dsaToast publisher, showing a 2.2s message with a ViShape glyph from a single shared instance.
intention_for_embedding: To let any code surface brief branded feedback with one global call, decoupled from the React tree.
